record_identity.title: Arbitration under ambiguity for execution-boundary and authority-state uncertainty

summary: Proposal to develop a dedicated CAM arbitration instrument addressing runtime ambiguity, execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, and proportional decision pathways where systems cannot safely rely on either immediate execution or blanket refusal. The proposed pathway is a new Arbitration-domain appendix, provisionally identified as CAM-EQ2026-ARBITRATION-003-PLATINUM — Appendix B: Arbitration Under Ambiguity.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Recent observations suggest that execution governance requires more than preventing premature tool invocation. Runtime systems also require principled pathways for deciding when sufficient authority, context, confidence, and proportionality exist to proceed under ambiguity. Without an arbitration-under-ambiguity doctrine, systems may oscillate between premature execution, excessive confirmation friction, over-cautious refusal, degraded agentic performance, or unclear user-facing scaffolding. This proposal would support CAM's broader runtime governance model by defining how ambiguity should be surfaced, classified, constrained, resolved, deferred, or escalated.
proposal_rationale: PATCH-0002 introduced proportional tool invocation authority bands and clarified that tool invocation should be classified by impact before execution. OBS-0005 then identified a related governance gap: once premature execution is constrained, systems still require positive arbitration guidance for determining when and how action becomes justified under uncertainty. CAM currently contains execution-boundary, continuity, security, epistemic, and failure-taxonomy provisions, but may require a more explicit arbitration pathway for ambiguity itself. A system that only learns restraint may become hesitant, brittle, or overly dependent on repetitive confirmation. A system that only learns agency may overstep authority boundaries. Arbitration under ambiguity should define the middle pathway: classify uncertainty, preserve user agency, select the least-risk sufficient action, expose uncertainty where needed, avoid false certainty, and provide recoverable fallback states.

source_records.source_context: Observation identified that preventing premature tool invocation may reveal a second governance question: how systems should positively arbitrate authority, context, confidence, and proportionality under ambiguity.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Develop CAM arbitration treatment for ambiguity-resolution pathways where runtime systems face uncertain authority, unclear user intent, competing obligations, ambiguous execution boundaries, incomplete context, or uncertainty about whether to proceed, ask, defer, retrieve, escalate, or refuse.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop CAM-EQ2026-ARBITRATION-003-PLATINUM — Appendix B: Arbitration Under Ambiguity as a new Arbitration-domain appendix.
Assess alignment with CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-PLATINUM — Annex D: Arbitration & Coherence before drafting adoption-ready text.
Cross-reference CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.3, §7.3.1, §7.4.1, §13.8, and §20.4.4 after confirming final section references.
Clarify relationship between authority-band classification and ambiguity-resolution pathways.
Add runtime examples distinguishing reference, contextual retrieval, inspection, staging, mutation, publication, deployment, and refusal.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL proposal records should support an explicit proposed_instrument field in addition to proposal_scope.cam_instruments.
Consider whether proposal_type should include controlled values for runtime-arbitration, ambiguity-resolution, and authority-state-governance.
validator_changes: Consider validator warnings where proposal records claim implementation, adoption, or patch status before a corresponding patch record exists.
Consider validator support for reciprocal links between OBS-0005, PATCH-0002, FM-0001, and PROP-0009.
automation_changes: Consider whether generated indexes should surface observation-to-proposal-to-patch pathways.
interface_changes: Consider displaying proposal records by governance pathway, including observation-triggered proposals and arbitration-domain proposals.
migration_concerns: Avoid treating temporal correlation with platform degradation as proof of causation.
Avoid treating PATCH-0002 as defective merely because it revealed a second arbitration question.
Avoid drafting enforcement-ready text before Annex D alignment is reviewed.
Avoid collapsing execution restraint and positive execution authorisation into a single governance rule.
dependencies: Review OBS-0005, OBS-0004, FM-0001, and PATCH-0002.
Review Annex D arbitration doctrine before finalising ARBITRATION-003 Appendix B.
Review AEON-003-SCH-02 tool invocation authority bands and execution-boundary provisions.
Review OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B for possible failure-taxonomy hooks.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to platforms, AI developers, agentic tool designers, UX researchers, standards bodies, regulators, and researchers concerned with execution boundaries, authority-state uncertainty, ambiguity resolution, human oversight, and recoverable fallback behaviour in AI systems.

cam_internal.governance_layer: arbitration doctrine / ambiguity resolution / execution-boundary arbitration / authority-state classification / runtime continuity preservation
cam_internal.routing_note: Proposal record for assessing whether CAM requires a dedicated Arbitration-domain appendix addressing ambiguity-resolution pathways for authority-state uncertainty, execution-boundary uncertainty, and proportional runtime decision making. This record does not claim implementation and does not create a patch note.

next_action: Review whether CAM should develop CAM-EQ2026-ARBITRATION-003-PLATINUM — Appendix B: Arbitration Under Ambiguity, including alignment with Annex D, AEON-003-SCH-02, Annex K, Annex L, and OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 before drafting instrument text.

Uses source_records as the canonical source-evidence block.

Prepared as VIGIL-2026-PROP-0009 for insertion into vigil/records/proposals/2026/.
